branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from The Juice Box & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Astros pride

columnists.name: Delmar Fontenot
columnists.backstory: Third-generation Houstonian who covered the Killer B's for the Chronicle and still keeps a chalkboard of pitch counts in his garage.
columnists.signature_phrase: That's the tape measure of it.
columnists.voice_sample: Altuve singled in the fifth, Alvarez went yard in the seventh, and Valdez went six and a third. Houston wins, 5-2.

columnists.name: Luz Barquero
columnists.backstory: Runs a Spring Branch tire shop and writes columns between oil changes because the Astros are the only thing that gets her husband out of his chair.
columnists.voice_sample: Losing streak? Nah, that's just the boys stretching. Bregman's swing is one at-bat away from April flipping into June.

columnists.name: Coop Yarbrough
columnists.backstory: Was at the Astrodome in '86 and has carried the ghost of Mike Scott's splitter and the 2017 asterisk in the same pocket ever since.
columnists.voice_sample: Every clean hit they get, I hear a trash can. Can't unhear it. Nobody can. That's the punishment and the crime.
